pg158-p2-s1: Next day found the prospect more receptive.
pg158-p2-s2:
He had been thinking it over. "Maybe you're right," he said. "God ought to
be able to do anything."
pg158-p2-s3:
Then he added, "He sure didn't do much for me when I was trying to fight this booze racket
alone."
pg158-p2:
Next day found the prospect more receptive. He had been thinking it over. "Maybe you're right," he said. "God ought to
be able to do anything." Then he added, "He sure didn't do much for me when I was trying to fight this booze racket
alone."
pg158-p3-s1:
On the third day the lawyer gave his life to the care and direction of his Creator, and said he was perfectly willing to
do anything necessary.
pg158-p3-s2:
His wife came, scarcely daring to be hopeful, though she thought she saw something different
about her husband already.
pg158-p3-s3: He had begun to have a spiritual experience.
pg158-p3:
On the third day the lawyer gave his life to the care and direction of his Creator, and said he was perfectly willing to
do anything necessary. His wife came, scarcely daring to be hopeful, though she thought she saw something different
about her husband already. He had begun to have a spiritual experience.
pg158-p4-s1: That afternoon he put on his clothes and walked from the hospital a free man.
pg158-p4-s2:
He entered a political campaign, making
speeches, frequenting men's gathering places of all sorts, often staying up all night.
pg158-p4-s3:
He lost the race by only a narrow
margin.
pg158-p4-s4: But he had found God - and in finding God had found himself.
pg158-p4:
That afternoon he put on his clothes and walked from the hospital a free man. He entered a political campaign, making
speeches, frequenting men's gathering places of all sorts, often staying up all night. He lost the race by only a narrow
margin. But he had found God - and in finding God had found himself.
pg158-p5-s3: He too, has become a respected and useful member of his community.
pg158-p5-s4:
He has
helped other men recover, and is a power in the church from which he was long absent.
pg158-p5:
That was in June, 1935. He never drank again. He too, has become a respected and useful member of his community. He has
helped other men recover, and is a power in the church from which he was long absent.
pg158-p6-s1:
So, you see, there were three alcoholics in that town, who now felt they had to give to others what they had found, or
be sunk.
pg158-p6-s2: After several failures to find others, a fourth turned up.
pg158-p6-s3:
He came through an acquaintance who had heard the
good news.
pg158-p6-s4:
He proved to be a devil-may-care young fellow whose parents could not make out whether he wanted to stop
drinking or not.
pg158-p6-s5: They were deeply religious people, much shocked by their son's refusal to have anything to do with the
pg158-p6:
So, you see, there were three alcoholics in that town, who now felt they had to give to others what they had found, or
be sunk. After several failures to find others, a fourth turned up. He came through an acquaintance who had heard the
good news. He proved to be a devil-may-care young fellow whose parents could not make out whether he wanted to stop
